questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph: In an organ pipe (may be closed or open) of 99 cm length standing wave is setup, whose equation is given by longitudinal displacement $\xi=(0.1 \mathrm{~mm}) \cos \frac{2 \pi}{0.8}(y+1 \mathrm{~cm}) \cos 2 \pi(400) \mathrm{t}$ where $y$ is measured from the top of the tube in meters and t in second. Here 1 cm is the end correction.
Question: Equation of the standing wave in terms of excess pressure is (Bulk modulus of air $\mathrm{B}=5 \times 10^5 \mathrm{~N} / \mathrm{m}^2$ )
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} P_{e x} & =-B \frac{d \xi}{d x} \\ & =\left(5 \times 10^5\right) \times\left(0.1 \times 10^{-3}\right) \frac{2 \pi}{0.8} \sin \frac{2 \pi}{0.8}(y+1 \mathrm{~cm}) \cos 2 \pi(400) t \\ & =\left(125 \pi N / m^2\right) \sin \frac{2 \pi}{0.8}(y+1 \mathrm{~cm}) \cos 2 \pi(400 \mathrm{t})\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph: The Doppler flow meter is a particularly interesting medical application of the Doppler effect. This device measures the speed of blood flow, using transmitting and receiving elements that are placed directly on the skin, as in Figure. The transmitter emits a continuous sound whose frequency is typically about 5 MHz . When the sound is reflected from the red blood cells, its frequency is changed in a kind of Doppler effect because the cells are moving with the same velocity as the blood. The receiving element detects the reflected sound, and an electronic counter measures its frequency, which is Doppler-shifted relative to the transmitter frequency. From the change in frequency the speed of the blood flow can be determined. Typically, the change in frequency is around 600 Hz for flow speeds of about $0.1 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$.
Question: An abnormal segment of the artery is narrowed down by an arteriosclerotic plaque to one-fourth the normal cross-sectional area. What will be the change in frequency due to reflection from the red blood cell in that region?
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph: The Doppler flow meter is a particularly interesting medical application of the Doppler effect. This device measures the speed of blood flow, using transmitting and receiving elements that are placed directly on the skin, as in Figure. The transmitter emits a continuous sound whose frequency is typically about 5 MHz . When the sound is reflected from the red blood cells, its frequency is changed in a kind of Doppler effect because the cells are moving with the same velocity as the blood. The receiving element detects the reflected sound, and an electronic counter measures its frequency, which is Doppler-shifted relative to the transmitter frequency. From the change in frequency the speed of the blood flow can be determined. Typically, the change in frequency is around 600 Hz for flow speeds of about $0.1 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$.
Question: At what extra rate does the heart have to work due to this narrowing down of the artery? Assume the density to be $1.5 \mathrm{gm} / \mathrm{cc}$ and the area of the normal artery to be $0.1 \mathrm{~cm}^2$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph: In an organ pipe (may be closed or open) of 99 cm length standing wave is setup, whose equation is given by longitudinal displacement $\xi=(0.1 \mathrm{~mm}) \cos \frac{2 \pi}{0.8}(y+1 \mathrm{~cm}) \cos 2 \pi(400) \mathrm{t}$ where $y$ is measured from the top of the tube in meters and t in second. Here 1 cm is the end correction.
Question: The air column is vibrating in
questions.solution: $\frac{2 \pi}{0.8}=\frac{2 \pi}{\lambda}$ So $\lambda=0.8$ and effective length of air column $=0.99+0.01=1 \mathrm{~m}$. $\frac{\ell}{\lambda}=\frac{5}{4}$ $1=5 \frac{\lambda}{4}$, so five half loops will be formed $\ell=5\left(\frac{\lambda}{4}\right)$ so second overtone
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph: The Doppler flow meter is a particularly interesting medical application of the Doppler effect. This device measures the speed of blood flow, using transmitting and receiving elements that are placed directly on the skin, as in Figure. The transmitter emits a continuous sound whose frequency is typically about 5 MHz . When the sound is reflected from the red blood cells, its frequency is changed in a kind of Doppler effect because the cells are moving with the same velocity as the blood. The receiving element detects the reflected sound, and an electronic counter measures its frequency, which is Doppler-shifted relative to the transmitter frequency. From the change in frequency the speed of the blood flow can be determined. Typically, the change in frequency is around 600 Hz for flow speeds of about $0.1 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$.
Question: Assume that the red blood cell is directly moving away from the source and the receiver. What is the (approx) speed of the sound wave in the blood?

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: At a time $t=0$ a pulse moving in the $+v e x$-direction with velocity v in the shape given by the function between with $y=\left\{\begin{array}{ll}\frac{x}{4}+1 & -4 \lt x \lt 0 \\ -x+1 & 0 \lt x \lt 1 \\ 0 & \text { otherwise }\end{array}\right.$, where x and y are in cm .Question: If velocity is $10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$, which of the graphs shows the shape and position of the string at a time $\mathrm{t}=$ 1 millisec
questions.solution:
At t $=0$
velocity of wave $=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$. Thus pulse get shifted by $\left(10 \times 10^{-3}\right) \mathrm{m}$
Wave equation; obtained by replacing $x$ by ( $x-v t$ ) Pulse reaches $\mathrm{x}=20 \mathrm{~m}$ at $\mathrm{t}=2 \mathrm{sec}$ First half of the wave reaches at $\mathrm{t}=1.999 \mathrm{sec} ; \mathrm{V}=$ particle $=\frac{d x}{d t}=v_{\text {wave }}=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$ Second half of the wave reaches at $\mathrm{t}=2 \&$ leaves at $\mathrm{t}=2.004 \mathrm{sec} ; \mathrm{V}=\frac{1}{4} \frac{d x}{d t}=-\frac{5}{2}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: At a time $t=0$ a pulse moving in the $+v e x$-direction with velocity v in the shape given by the function between with $y=\left\{\begin{array}{ll}\frac{x}{4}+1 & -4 \lt x \lt 0 \\ -x+1 & 0 \lt x \lt 1 \\ 0 & \text { otherwise }\end{array}\right.$, where x and y are in cm .Question: The wave equation that explicitly gives the displacement of any point at any time is

At t $=0$
velocity of wave $=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$. Thus pulse get shifted by $\left(10 \times 10^{-3}\right) \mathrm{m}$
Wave equation; obtained by replacing $x$ by ( $x-v t$ ) Pulse reaches $\mathrm{x}=20 \mathrm{~m}$ at $\mathrm{t}=2 \mathrm{sec}$ First half of the wave reaches at $\mathrm{t}=1.999 \mathrm{sec} ; \mathrm{V}=$ particle $=\frac{d x}{d t}=v_{\text {wave }}=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$ Second half of the wave reaches at $\mathrm{t}=2 \&$ leaves at $\mathrm{t}=2.004 \mathrm{sec} ; \mathrm{V}=\frac{1}{4} \frac{d x}{d t}=-\frac{5}{2}$

questions.subject: Physics

questions.q: Paragraph: At a time $t=0$ a pulse moving in the $+v e x$-direction with velocity v in the shape given by the function between with $y=\left\{\begin{array}{ll}\frac{x}{4}+1 & -4 \lt x \lt 0 \\ -x+1 & 0 \lt x \lt 1 \\ 0 & \text { otherwise }\end{array}\right.$, where x and y are in cm .Question: Velocity of a point at $x=20 \mathrm{~m}$ as a function of time is given as
questions.solution:

velocity of wave $=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$. Thus pulse get shifted by $\left(10 \times 10^{-3}\right) \mathrm{m}$
Wave equation; obtained by replacing $x$ by ( $x-v t$ ) Pulse reaches $\mathrm{x}=20 \mathrm{~m}$ at $\mathrm{t}=2 \mathrm{sec}$ First half of the wave reaches at $\mathrm{t}=1.999 \mathrm{sec} ; \mathrm{V}=$ particle $=\frac{d x}{d t}=v_{\text {wave }}=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$ Second half of the wave reaches at $\mathrm{t}=2 \&$ leaves at $\mathrm{t}=2.004 \mathrm{sec} ; \mathrm{V}=\frac{1}{4} \frac{d x}{d t}=-\frac{5}{2}$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: A metallic rod of length 1 m has one end free and other end rigidly clamped. Longitudinal stationary waves are set up in the rod in such a way that there are total six antinodes present along the rod. The amplitude of an antinode is $4 \times 10^{-6} \mathrm{~m}$. Young's modulus and density of the rod are $6.4 \times 10^{10} \mathrm{~N} / \mathrm{m}^2$ and $4 \times 10^3 \mathrm{Kg} / \mathrm{m}^3$ respectively. Consider the free end to be at origin and at $\mathrm{t}=0$ particles at free end are at positive extreme.Question: The equation describing stress developed in the rod is
questions.solution:
Speed of wave $v=\sqrt{\frac{y}{\rho}}=4 \times 10^3$
$l=\frac{5 \lambda}{2}+\frac{\lambda}{4} \Rightarrow \lambda=\frac{4 \ell}{11}$ Frequency $v=\frac{v}{\lambda}=\frac{4 \times 10^3}{\frac{4}{11} \times 1}=11 \times 10^3 \mathrm{~Hz}$; Wave Number $K=\frac{2 \pi}{\lambda}=\frac{11 \pi}{2}$ (i) Equation of standing wave in the rod $\begin{aligned} & S=A \operatorname{coskx} \sin (\omega \mathrm{t}+\phi) \text { where } \mathrm{A}=4 \times 10^{-6} \mathrm{~m} \quad \because \text { at } \mathrm{x}=0, \mathrm{t}=0 \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{~S}=\mathrm{A} \Rightarrow \mathrm{~A}=\mathrm{A} \Rightarrow \operatorname{cosk}(0) \sin \phi \Rightarrow \sin \phi=1 \Rightarrow \phi=\frac{\pi}{2} \\ & S=4 \times 10^{-6} \cos \left(\frac{11 \pi}{2} x\right) \cos \left(22 \pi \times 10^3 t\right) \end{aligned}$ $\begin{aligned} & \text {(ii)} \text { Strain }=\frac{d s}{d x}=-22 \pi \times 10^{-6} \sin \left(\frac{11 \pi}{2} x\right) \cos \left(22 \pi \times 10^3 t\right) \because \text { stress }=Y \times \text { strain } \\ & \Rightarrow \text { stress }=140.8 \times 10^4 \cos \left(22 \pi \times 10^3 t\right) \sin \left(\frac{11 \pi}{2} x+\pi\right) \end{aligned}$ (iii) Strain at $\mathrm{t}=1 \mathrm{~s}$ and $\mathrm{x}=\frac{\ell}{2}=\frac{1}{2} m$ $\left|\frac{d s}{d x}\right|_{x=\frac{\ell}{2}}^{l=1}=22 \pi \times 10^{-6} \times \sin \left(\frac{11 \pi}{4}\right)=11 \sqrt{2} \pi \times 10^{-6}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: A metallic rod of length 1 m has one end free and other end rigidly clamped. Longitudinal stationary waves are set up in the rod in such a way that there are total six antinodes present along the rod. The amplitude of an antinode is $4 \times 10^{-6} \mathrm{~m}$. Young's modulus and density of the rod are $6.4 \times 10^{10} \mathrm{~N} / \mathrm{m}^2$ and $4 \times 10^3 \mathrm{Kg} / \mathrm{m}^3$ respectively. Consider the free end to be at origin and at $\mathrm{t}=0$ particles at free end are at positive extreme.Question: The magnitude of strain at midpoint of the rod at $t=1 \mathrm{sec}$ is
questions.solution:
Speed of wave $v=\sqrt{\frac{y}{\rho}}=4 \times 10^3$
$l=\frac{5 \lambda}{2}+\frac{\lambda}{4} \Rightarrow \lambda=\frac{4 \ell}{11}$ Frequency $v=\frac{v}{\lambda}=\frac{4 \times 10^3}{\frac{4}{11} \times 1}=11 \times 10^3 \mathrm{~Hz}$; Wave Number $K=\frac{2 \pi}{\lambda}=\frac{11 \pi}{2}$ (i) Equation of standing wave in the rod $\begin{aligned} & S=A \operatorname{coskx} \sin (\omega \mathrm{t}+\phi) \text { where } \mathrm{A}=4 \times 10^{-6} \mathrm{~m} \quad \because \text { at } \mathrm{x}=0, \mathrm{t}=0 \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{~S}=\mathrm{A} \Rightarrow \mathrm{~A}=\mathrm{A} \Rightarrow \operatorname{cosk}(0) \sin \phi \Rightarrow \sin \phi=1 \Rightarrow \phi=\frac{\pi}{2} \\ & S=4 \times 10^{-6} \cos \left(\frac{11 \pi}{2} x\right) \cos \left(22 \pi \times 10^3 t\right) \end{aligned}$ $\begin{aligned} & \text {(ii)} \text { Strain }=\frac{d s}{d x}=-22 \pi \times 10^{-6} \sin \left(\frac{11 \pi}{2} x\right) \cos \left(22 \pi \times 10^3 t\right) \because \text { stress }=Y \times \text { strain } \\ & \Rightarrow \text { stress }=140.8 \times 10^4 \cos \left(22 \pi \times 10^3 t\right) \sin \left(\frac{11 \pi}{2} x+\pi\right) \end{aligned}$ (iii) Strain at $\mathrm{t}=1 \mathrm{~s}$ and $\mathrm{x}=\frac{\ell}{2}=\frac{1}{2} m$ $\left|\frac{d s}{d x}\right|_{x=\frac{\ell}{2}}^{l=1}=22 \pi \times 10^{-6} \times \sin \left(\frac{11 \pi}{4}\right)=11 \sqrt{2} \pi \times 10^{-6}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Sinusoidal waves travel on five identical strings. Use the mathematical forms of the waves given below. In the expressions given below x is in m and y is in millimeters and t is in seconds. (a)$\mathrm{y}=(2 \mathrm{~mm}) \sin (2 \mathrm{x}-4 \mathrm{t})$ (b)$y=(3 m m) \sin (4 x-10 t)$ (c)$y=(2.5 \mathrm{~mm}) \sin (6 x-12 t)$ (d)$y=(1 m m) \sin (8 x-16 t)$ (e) $y=(4 m m) \sin (10 x-20 t)$Question: Four of the strings have the same tension, but the fifth has a different tension. Identify it.
questions.solution:
(i) $\mathrm{v}_1=\frac{\mathrm{w}}{\mathrm{k}}=\frac{4}{2}=2 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{v}_2=\frac{10}{4}=2.5 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s} \\
& \mathrm{v}_3=\frac{12}{6}=2 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s} \\
& \mathrm{v}_4=\frac{16}{8}=2 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s} \\
& \mathrm{v}_5=\frac{20}{10}=2 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s} \\
& \mathrm{v}=\sqrt{\frac{\mathrm{T}}{\mu}} \text { so } \mathrm{T}_2 \text { is diff. }
\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned}
& \text { (ii) } \mathrm{E}_1=\frac{1}{2} \mu \mathrm{vA} \mathrm{~A}^2 \mathrm{w}^2 \mathrm{~T} \\
& \frac{1}{2} \mu \mathrm{vA} \mathrm{~A}^2 \times 4 \pi \mathrm{f}^{\prime} \mathrm{t}=2 \mu \mathrm{v} \times \mathrm{A}^2 \pi^2 \mathrm{f} \\
& \mathrm{E}_1=2 \mu \mathrm{v} \times 2^2 \pi \times 4
\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{E}_2=2 \mu \mathrm{v} \times 3^2 \times \pi \times 10 \\
& \mathrm{E}_3=2 \mu \mathrm{v} \times 2.5^2 \times \pi \times 12 \\
& \mathrm{E}_4=2 \mu \mathrm{v} \times 1^2 \times \pi \times 16 \\
& \mathrm{E}_5=2 \mu \mathrm{v} \times 4^2 \times \pi \times 20
\end{aligned}$

So $\mathrm{E}_1 \& \mathrm{E}_4$ are same
(iii) slope $\mathrm{BA} \cos (\mathrm{kx}-\mathrm{wt})$
$\begin{aligned}
& \text { slope }_1=2 \times 2 \cos (2 \times 8-4 \times 4) \\
& \text { slope }_2=3 \times 4 \cos (6 \times 8-10 \times 4) \\
& \text { slope }_3=2.5 \times 6 \cos (6 \times 8-12 \times 4) \\
& \text { slope }_4=1 \times 8 \cos (8 \times 8-16 \times 4) \\
& \text { slope }_5=4 \times 10 \cos (10 \times 8-20 \times 4) \\
& \text { slope }_5 \text { is max. }
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Paragraph: Sinusoidal waves travel on five identical strings. Use the mathematical forms of the waves given below. In the expressions given below x is in m and y is in millimeters and t is in seconds. (a)$\mathrm{y}=(2 \mathrm{~mm}) \sin (2 \mathrm{x}-4 \mathrm{t})$ (b)$y=(3 m m) \sin (4 x-10 t)$ (c)$y=(2.5 \mathrm{~mm}) \sin (6 x-12 t)$ (d)$y=(1 m m) \sin (8 x-16 t)$ (e) $y=(4 m m) \sin (10 x-20 t)$Question: Two of the strings have the same energy in one wavelength, Identify the pair.
questions.solution:
(i) $\mathrm{v}_1=\frac{\mathrm{w}}{\mathrm{k}}=\frac{4}{2}=2 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{v}_2=\frac{10}{4}=2.5 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s} \\
& \mathrm{v}_3=\frac{12}{6}=2 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s} \\
& \mathrm{v}_4=\frac{16}{8}=2 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s} \\
& \mathrm{v}_5=\frac{20}{10}=2 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s} \\
& \mathrm{v}=\sqrt{\frac{\mathrm{T}}{\mu}} \text { so } \mathrm{T}_2 \text { is diff. }
\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned}
& \text { (ii) } \mathrm{E}_1=\frac{1}{2} \mu \mathrm{vA} \mathrm{~A}^2 \mathrm{w}^2 \mathrm{~T} \\
& \frac{1}{2} \mu \mathrm{vA} \mathrm{~A}^2 \times 4 \pi \mathrm{f}^{\prime} \mathrm{t}=2 \mu \mathrm{v} \times \mathrm{A}^2 \pi^2 \mathrm{f} \\
& \mathrm{E}_1=2 \mu \mathrm{v} \times 2^2 \pi \times 4
\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{E}_2=2 \mu \mathrm{v} \times 3^2 \times \pi \times 10 \\
& \mathrm{E}_3=2 \mu \mathrm{v} \times 2.5^2 \times \pi \times 12 \\
& \mathrm{E}_4=2 \mu \mathrm{v} \times 1^2 \times \pi \times 16 \\
& \mathrm{E}_5=2 \mu \mathrm{v} \times 4^2 \times \pi \times 20
\end{aligned}$

So $\mathrm{E}_1 \& \mathrm{E}_4$ are same
(iii) slope $\mathrm{BA} \cos (\mathrm{kx}-\mathrm{wt})$
$\begin{aligned}
& \text { slope }_1=2 \times 2 \cos (2 \times 8-4 \times 4) \\
& \text { slope }_2=3 \times 4 \cos (6 \times 8-10 \times 4) \\
& \text { slope }_3=2.5 \times 6 \cos (6 \times 8-12 \times 4) \\
& \text { slope }_4=1 \times 8 \cos (8 \times 8-16 \times 4) \\
& \text { slope }_5=4 \times 10 \cos (10 \times 8-20 \times 4) \\
& \text { slope }_5 \text { is max. }
\end{aligned}$

$\begin{aligned}

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: A metallic rod of length 1 m has one end free and other end rigidly clamped. Longitudinal stationary waves are set up in the rod in such a way that there are total six antinodes present along the rod. The amplitude of an antinode is $4 \times 10^{-6} \mathrm{~m}$. Young's modulus and density of the rod are $6.4 \times 10^{10} \mathrm{~N} / \mathrm{m}^2$ and $4 \times 10^3 \mathrm{Kg} / \mathrm{m}^3$ respectively. Consider the free end to be at origin and at $\mathrm{t}=0$ particles at free end are at positive extreme.Question: The equation describing displacements of particles about their mean positions is
questions.solution:
Speed of wave $v=\sqrt{\frac{y}{\rho}}=4 \times 10^3$
$l=\frac{5 \lambda}{2}+\frac{\lambda}{4} \Rightarrow \lambda=\frac{4 \ell}{11}$ Frequency $v=\frac{v}{\lambda}=\frac{4 \times 10^3}{\frac{4}{11} \times 1}=11 \times 10^3 \mathrm{~Hz}$; Wave Number $K=\frac{2 \pi}{\lambda}=\frac{11 \pi}{2}$ (i) Equation of standing wave in the rod $\begin{aligned} & S=A \operatorname{coskx} \sin (\omega \mathrm{t}+\phi) \text { where } \mathrm{A}=4 \times 10^{-6} \mathrm{~m} \quad \because \text { at } \mathrm{x}=0, \mathrm{t}=0 \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{~S}=\mathrm{A} \Rightarrow \mathrm{~A}=\mathrm{A} \Rightarrow \operatorname{cosk}(0) \sin \phi \Rightarrow \sin \phi=1 \Rightarrow \phi=\frac{\pi}{2} \\ & S=4 \times 10^{-6} \cos \left(\frac{11 \pi}{2} x\right) \cos \left(22 \pi \times 10^3 t\right) \end{aligned}$ $\begin{aligned} & \text {(ii)} \text { Strain }=\frac{d s}{d x}=-22 \pi \times 10^{-6} \sin \left(\frac{11 \pi}{2} x\right) \cos \left(22 \pi \times 10^3 t\right) \because \text { stress }=Y \times \text { strain } \\ & \Rightarrow \text { stress }=140.8 \times 10^4 \cos \left(22 \pi \times 10^3 t\right) \sin \left(\frac{11 \pi}{2} x+\pi\right) \end{aligned}$ (iii) Strain at $\mathrm{t}=1 \mathrm{~s}$ and $\mathrm{x}=\frac{\ell}{2}=\frac{1}{2} m$ $\left|\frac{d s}{d x}\right|_{x=\frac{\ell}{2}}^{l=1}=22 \pi \times 10^{-6} \times \sin \left(\frac{11 \pi}{4}\right)=11 \sqrt{2} \pi \times 10^{-6}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
